title: (No AI) Tidal won’t pay royalties on AI-generated music but isn’t banning it outright
author: Andrew Liszewski
content:
![Colorful graphic of Tidal logo](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/chorus/uploads/chorus_asset/file/25319678/STK301_Tidal_01.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Tidal [shared its new policies regarding AI-generated music today](https://tidal.com/ai-policy) and how the platform plans to "protect artists" and "inform listeners." Instead of banning it outright, [starting on July 15th](https://x.com/TIDAL/status/2071593228952822146) Tidal will label tracks it has identified as being 100 percent AI-generated with an icon. But starting today those tracks will no longer be monetizable. "Tidal's priority is ensuring royalties go to original works directly produced, written, and performed by people. We will therefore not knowingly attribute royalties to music we identify as wholly AI-generated," the company's announcement reads.

The platform didn't specify what tools it's using to iden …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/959211/tidal-ai-music-policy-demonetizingdetect-label)
contentSnippet:
Tidal shared its new policies regarding AI-generated music today and how the platform plans to "protect artists" and "inform listeners." Instead of banning it outright, starting on July 15th Tidal will label tracks it has identified as being 100 percent AI-generated with an icon. But starting today those tracks will no longer be monetizable. "Tidal's priority is ensuring royalties go to original works directly produced, written, and performed by people. We will therefore not knowingly attribute royalties to music we identify as wholly AI-generated," the company's announcement reads. 
The platform didn't specify what tools it's using to iden …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) OpenAI is teasing new hardware… for Codex
author: Emma Roth
content:
![An image of OpenAI’s upcoming Codex device](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/openai-codex-hardware.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

OpenAI is releasing some sort of device related to its AI-powered coding tool, Codex, on July 15th. In [a video posted to X](https://x.com/OpenAIDevs/status/2071639953927438440) on Monday, OpenAI shows a square-shaped device with several buttons, alongside the caption, "Your favorite Codex shortcuts are getting an upgrade."

This isn't the mysterious [AI-powered device OpenAI](https://www.theverge.com/news/827607/openai-hardware-prototype-chatgpt-jony-ive-sam-altman) is working on with former Apple designer Jony Ive, however. As shown in the teaser, OpenAI is launching the device in partnership with Work Louder, a company that sells an array of mechanical keyboards and macro pads with mappable keys, dials, and switches.

The silhouette of the device shown by OpenAI looks a bit [like Work …](https://worklouder.cc/creator-micro-2)

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/959174/openai-codex-hardware-work-louder)

The silhouette of the device shown by OpenAI looks a bit like Work  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

creator: Katie Drummond
title: (No AI) Bernie Sanders Saw This Coming
dc:creator: Katie Drummond
content: For decades, the senator has argued that concentrated wealth threatened American democracy. Now he’s betting that frustration with Big Tech, billionaires, and unchecked AI is reaching a tipping point.
contentSnippet: For decades, the senator has argued that concentrated wealth threatened American democracy. Now he’s betting that frustration with Big Tech, billionaires, and unchecked AI is reaching a tipping point.

creator: Dhruv Mehrotra, Joel Khalili
title: (No AI) Meta Contractors Posed as Teens to Prompt Rival Chatbots About Suicide, Sex, and Drugs
dc:creator: Dhruv Mehrotra, Joel Khalili
content: Hundreds of contractors working on a project for Meta pretended to be kids in order to see how other chatbots like Gemini and ChatGPT would respond to high-risk subjects, WIRED found.
contentSnippet: Hundreds of contractors working on a project for Meta pretended to be kids in order to see how other chatbots like Gemini and ChatGPT would respond to high-risk subjects, WIRED found.
